chunks.text: Sophia Shao is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS department. Her contact information includes 570 Cory Hall and email ysshao@berkeley.edu. Links to her personal homepage, publications, dissertations, and research support are provided, with Christina Hamilton as a contact at cchamilton@berkeley.edu.

Sophia Shao earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Harvard University in 2016. Her research areas include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Integrated Circuits (INC), and Cyber-Physical Systems and Design Automation (CPSDA).

Sophia Shao is involved in several research centers: Agile Design of Efficient Processing Technologies (ADEPT), Berkeley Emerging Technologies Research (BETR), Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC), and SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE). She teaches EE 194. IC Design Project: 16nm SoC for IoT on TuTh 11:00-12:29 in Cory 540AB and EE 197-30. Field Study.

Sophia Shao has received numerous honors and awards, including Sloan Research Fellow (2024), Anita Borg Early Career Award (2024), NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (2023), Google Faculty Research Award (2023), Okawa Research Grant (2022), IEEE TCCA Young Computer Architect Award (2022), and Intel Rising Star Faculty Award (2022).
